# continuous-learning — LLM Reference

Extracts reusable patterns from sessions and routes them to the correct memory topic file. Fires automatically at session end via Stop hook. Also available manually mid-session.

## Triggers — use /learn when

- Just solved something non-trivial — error resolution, debugging technique, workaround
- Discovered a project-specific pattern Claude didn't know about
- Made a correction to Claude's default approach that should be remembered
- Any Stop hook firing (automatic — runs evaluate-session.sh)

## Invocation

```
/learn                         # extract patterns from current session immediately
```

Automatic trigger: Stop hook runs evaluate-session.sh on every session end.

## What it does

1. Evaluates the session for patterns worth extracting
2. Routes each pattern to the correct topic file (see routing table below)
3. Deduplicates — updates existing entries rather than appending duplicates

## Routing table

| Content type | Target file |
|---|---|
| Hook fix, hook config change | `<global-memory>/topics/hooks.md` |
| Project scaffold, stack decision | `<global-memory>/topics/scaffold.md` |
| Agent delegation, model selection | `<global-memory>/topics/agents.md` |
| Bug fix insight, debugging technique | `<global-memory>/topics/debugging.md` |
| API design, architecture, code pattern | `<global-memory>/topics/patterns.md` |
| Project history, per-project decision | `<global-memory>/topics/projects.md` |
| Cross-project, multi-topic insight | `<global-memory>/insights.md` |
| User preference, workflow change | `<global-memory>/MEMORY.md` |

Global memory path: `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-home>/memory/` where `<encoded-home>` = `$HOME` with `/` → `-`.

## Key files

Write: `~/.claude/skills/learned/[pattern-name].md` (novel patterns as standalone skills)
Write: topic files under global memory (routed by content type above)
Read: session transcript (current conversation)

## What it doesn't do

- Never writes domain-specific content to MEMORY.md — that is the index only
- Never appends duplicate entries — always updates existing before adding new
